intro: Ostericum melanotilingia, also known as Korean angelica or yuchun, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Apiaceae family. It has been traditionally used in East Asian medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antispasmodic properties, which are believed to help alleviate symptoms of menstrual cramps, fever, and respiratory disorders. The bioactive constituents of Ostericum melanotilingia include flavonoids, terpenoids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ostericum melanotilingia is often prepared as a decoction, tea, or tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
